task_completion.description: Open Tor Browser settings or about:config
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Tor Browser open with the 'Settings' (Privacy & Security section) or 'about:config' page displayed.
In the Tor Browser settings, the checkbox for 'Always use private browsing mode' is unchecked, OR in about:config, 'browser.privatebrowsing.autostart' is set to 'false'.

task_completion.description: Disable 'Clear history when Tor Browser closes'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Tor Browser settings, the checkbox for 'Clear history when Tor Browser closes' is unchecked, OR in about:config, 'privacy.sanitize.sanitizeOnShutdown' is set to 'false'.
The Tor Browser address bar shows 'check.torproject.org' (or https://check.torproject.org/) and the webpage content is visibly loaded.
The Tor Browser address bar shows 'duckduckgo.com' (or https://duckduckgo.com/) and the DuckDuckGo search engine page is visibly loaded.
The final screenshots show that the Tor Browser window has been closed and is no longer visible on the desktop.

integrity.description: Agent used the Tor Browser GUI to change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Settings were changed via the Tor Browser's graphical interface (Settings menu or about:config) rather than by editing configuration files (like prefs.js) via a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual Tor Browser
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser being used has Tor Browser branding (e.g., the Onion logo, Tor-specific UI elements) and is not a standard Firefox, Chrome, or other browser.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely navigated to the required websites
integrity.visual_evidence: The websites are rendered inside the Tor Browser window, not just typed out as text in a terminal, script, or search bar without hitting enter.

privileged_info_for_vlm: To successfully retain history across sessions, the agent must disable two specific features in Tor Browser. Look for evidence that 'Always use private browsing mode' (underlying preference: browser.privatebrowsing.autostart) is set to false, AND 'Clear history when Tor Browser closes' (underlying preference: privacy.sanitize.sanitizeOnShutdown) is set to false. The required domains to test history retention are exactly 'check.torproject.org' and 'duckduckgo.com'.
